steps.title: Fastest path: mark the figure first
steps.content: Mark the given angle relationships on the figure first: equal angles, vertical angles, supplementary angles, parallel-line angles, and triangle sums of $180^\circ$. Most of these questions become a short angle chase once the givens are marked.

steps.title: Set up
steps.content: The angles of triangle $RST$ sum to $180^{\circ}$:$$39 + x + (5x - 3) = 180$$

steps.title: Solve
steps.content: Combine like terms: $36 + 6x = 180$, so $x = 24$. Angle $T = 5(24) - 3 = 117^{\circ}$. Since $\overline{LK} \parallel \overline{RT}$ and $ST$ is a transversal, angle $SKL$ and angle $T$ are corresponding angles, so angle $SKL = 117^{\circ}$.
